dimensions.rationale: Plan revision 11 implements specification revision 10 and narrows release scope to offline release-candidate readiness.
Portable core remains separate from adapters; release tooling is local and does not import project-specific state.
Controller preserve-compatible changes are lead-owned refreeze support; WS-14 owns only release/governance outputs and prior accepted workstream contracts remain unchanged.
External authority requirements are not weakened into false PASS claims; they are deferred blockers for production promotion.
WS-14 validation commands are local-process commands and individual external matrix legs are no longer required for offline candidate acceptance.
The blocked run can adopt revision 11 with preserveAcceptedCompatible and continue from WS-14.
The refreeze avoids rerunning already accepted compatible tasks and removes nine external matrix-leg host-operation validations from the required local candidate path.

findings.message: Revision 10 blocked autonomous completion because WS-14 required unavailable external CI, signature and live-cost authorities and pointed at non-existing release/final controller commands.
findings.requiredAction: Revision 11 shall only claim local offline release-candidate readiness, keep production promotion blocked until signed external evidence exists, and keep controller refreeze support lead-owned rather than assigning overlapping worker ownership.

summary: Revision 11 is READY_TO_FREEZE for autonomous offline release-candidate completion. Production promotion remains a separate deferred authority gate and is not claimed by this plan.
